Number one, we don't want to sound like everyone else that cold calls because you're not the— probably more than likely— the only person that's ever cold-called this prospect. And your prospect's brain, what happens is it picks up on, come over here for a second, it picks up on patterns. Okay, I probably spelled that wrong. Full disclosure. picks up the pattern of what most salespeople sound like. Hi, is this John? Hi, John. My name is Jeremy Miner with XYZ Company. So, if you have a monotone tonality, it picks up the pattern. Salesperson trying to sell.
me something. If you're too enthusiastic, like you're overboard, like, 'Hey, how's it going?' They pick up on that because that sounds like most salespeople. Or if you sound timid, if you sound like you have anxiety, nervousness that triggers a nervous system to go into fight or flight mode. So you don't want to do that. Okay. So you don't want to say, 'Hi, my name is— I'm with XYZ Company. The reason why I called.' was salesperson or 'Hi, is this John? Hey, John, this is Jeremy Miller with XYZ coming.' The average doctor in a hospital, typically, it takes them about 90 minutes to two hours each day to manually dictate the notes. They have to do that because if they do it wrong. Insurance companies won't pay them on time, which is a no bueno, not good. And second of all, they could prescribe the wrong medication, patient could die. So doctors have spent a lot of their day doing that. Now, if they're spending two hours a day manually dictating their notes, means they're going to do it later at night. So they're not seeing their family. They're getting burned out or they're doing it to work hours. That means they see less patients, less billable hours. Hospitals make less money. Okay. Hard for them to be against something. What's a safe word on a cold call that prospects want to use? The word 'no.' You're against? It's hard to say. Yes, I'm against. Having a brief conversation around. Possibly getting an offer on that home. Would you be opposed to that? Hard for them to say. Yes, I'd be opposed. You don't want to say 'open.' Would you be open? Because now 'yes' is a dangerous word for them. They don't like to say the word 'yes.' They're more comfortable with the use of the word 'no.' And 'no,' in this context, is really a 'yes' for you.
